description: Complete rebuild of RightHeadPanel.tsx with a production-grade face mesh visualization using layered contour rings and isoparametric curves approach (topographic contour lines on a face shape) rather than filled triangles. This creates a technical/engineering look while appearing clearly human and volumetric.
steps: Completely rewrite src/components/cockpit/RightHeadPanel.tsx with a new visual approach: Use SVG with layered concentric contour rings that follow the shape of a human face (like topographic elevation maps). The approach should be: (A) Define a set of contour paths at different 'depth levels' - front face center has tightest contours, edges have wider spacing. (B) Include distinct regions: forehead (horizontal curves), eye sockets (elliptical depressions), nose (central ridge with nostril contours), cheeks (smooth curved contours), jawline (outer boundary), chin, lips/mouth (small contour detail), neck, and subtle shoulder lines. (C) Use 5-7 concentric depth layers with decreasing opacity from center to edges. (D) Add a subtle triangulated wireframe OVERLAY (not as the primary visual) using thin lines connecting contour intersection points.

Implement proper 3D rotation: (A) Keep CSS perspective transform for the SVG container (perspective: 800px). (B) Apply rotateY(clampedYaw) rotateX(-clampedPitch) rotateZ(clampedRoll) with visual clamping at +-35 degrees. (C) Use smooth transitions (0.15s ease-out). (D) Set transform-origin to center. (E) Ensure the head is properly centered with adequate padding so no clipping occurs at any rotation angle.

Build the face mesh SVG with these visual layers (bottom to top): (1) Outermost face/head silhouette path as a subtle boundary. (2) Major contour rings (5-7 levels) using stroke-only paths with the dms-accent color (#00d4ff) at varying opacities (0.15 to 0.5 from outer to inner). (3) Feature-specific detail contours for eyes, nose, mouth, ears. (4) A very subtle fill gradient (radial, from center) to give depth perception. (5) Vertex/intersection dots at key landmarks (eyes, nose tip, chin) with subtle glow.

Implement the unified gaze cone visualization: (A) Use a cone from the midpoint between both eyes (cx=center, cy=eye_level). (B) The cone spreads outward in the gaze direction. (C) Color it green (#00e676) when onRoad, orange (#ff6b35) when off road. (D) Make it semi-transparent with gradient fade. (E) Add a thin center line within the cone for precision indication.

Add eye glow indicators: (A) Both eyes should have subtle pulsing glow circles. (B) Color them cyan/green when eyes are visible, dim when not. (C) Add a very subtle connecting bridge between eyes for the gaze origin point.

Keep the status info panel below the SVG showing: (A) Gaze: Road Center / Off Road status. (B) 0-axis calibrated label. (C) Road-facing neutral label. (D) Head Pose Valid / Lost. (E) Tracking Locked / Lost. (F) Eye openness L/R.
Keep the YAW/PITCH/ROLL numeric readout row at the bottom with the same font-mono styling.

Ensure the SVG viewBox and preserveAspectRatio are set so the face model is fully visible, centered, with no clipping. Use viewBox='0 0 200 280' or similar with xMidYMid meet. The model should use roughly 70-80% of the available space to leave padding for rotation.

The overall panel layout should remain: header row (title + gaze status badge), flex-1 SVG container, status info grid, YAW/PITCH/ROLL row. The panel border should glow cyan when onRoad, orange when off road.
acceptance_criteria: RightHeadPanel.tsx renders a face visualization using layered contour lines/rings approach - no filled triangle polygon mesh as the primary visual
The face clearly reads as human with identifiable forehead, eyes, nose, mouth, jawline, and neck regions
The face is fully visible within the panel at all rotation angles up to +-35 degrees - no clipping
Head visually rotates in response to yaw/pitch/roll data with smooth CSS transitions
Gaze cone renders from midpoint between eyes, colored by onRoad status
Raw YAW/PITCH/ROLL numeric values are displayed
Panel border glows cyan for onRoad, orange for off road
No new npm dependencies used - pure SVG + CSS + React
No TypeScript errors (verified by code inspection)
verification: Read RightHeadPanel.tsx and verify it does not contain FACE_MESH_TRIANGLES array or filled polygon-based rendering
Verify the SVG contains path elements with contour/ring patterns rather than polygon elements
Verify the component accepts DMSTelemetry data prop and renders headPose.yaw/pitch/roll values
Grep for 'preserveAspectRatio' in RightHeadPanel.tsx to confirm proper SVG scaling
findings: Complete rewrite successful. Used 7 contour layers with path elements (no polygons for face mesh). All acceptance criteria met via code inspection: contour paths define human facial topology, preserveAspectRatio set, CSS perspective rotation clamped at +-35 deg, gaze cone from eye midpoint, panel border glow, status panel, YPR readout. No TypeScript issues - all variables used, imports correct, types match.
